t_ch: 具体的坐标我晚点发给你。
t_en: I'll send you the exact coordinates later.

t_ch: 醒来之后的实验室位置。
t_en: Where was the lab you woke up in?

t_ch: 这个我可以告诉你详细的位置，故事里也是有伏笔的。因为终端的功能完善，所以我可以清晰地定位。嘿嘿，我严谨吧？
t_en: I can tell you exactly where it is, and there's foreshadowing in the story too. Since the terminal's functions were all intact, I could locate that place precisely. Hehe, aren't I meticulous?

t_ch: 我把具体的位置发给你，但是那里可能有人正在工作，进去的时候注意一下。
t_en: I'll send you the exact location, but there might be people working there. Just be mindful when you go in.

t_ch: 有没有其他细节？
t_en: Any other details?

t_ch: 非要说细节的话…或许是周围环境很暗？而且感觉自己头重脚轻…哎呀，好像都是我熬夜之后带来的坏毛病，不算细节，当不了伏笔。
t_en: Details... Maybe it was just really dark? And I felt lightheaded... But those are probably the results of me staying up all night. They aren't details that work as foreshadowing.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 我也没什么好说的了。
t_en: I don't have anything else to say.

t_ch: 我大概了解了。
t_en: I think I get it now.

t_ch: 对了，你跟阿德勒说一声，之后要是查到什么了记得先告诉我。
t_en: Right, tell Adler that if he finds anything, he needs to let me know first thing.

t_ch: 我可是把这个故事的改编权交给了他，总得有点回报……
t_en: I gave him the right to adapt this story. He's gotta give me something in return...

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: …别紧张，我只是在测试这玩意的重力感应系统。
t_en: ...Don't worry. I'm just testing its gravity sensor system.

t_ch: 「这个奇物」是哪个奇物？
t_en: Which Curio is "this Curio"?

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 说出来你别笑…就是我刚刚坐着的…「马桶」。
t_en: Well, just don't laugh... It's... the toilet seat I was sitting on.

t_ch: 你有见过一个神秘的马桶吗？
t_en: Have you seen a mysterious toilet seat?

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: …你这不是废话嘛，眼前这个就是啊！
t_en: ...What are you talking about? The thing in front of you is the Curio!

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 哎，你也是来找「实验品<unbreak>13</unbreak>号」的吧？就是那个长得像马桶的奇物。
t_en: Ah, you're here for Experiment <unbreak>13</unbreak> as well, right? The Curio that looks like a toilet seat?

t_ch: 对啊，你知道它的来历？
t_en: Yes. You know what it is?

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 对啊，它本来是我们科室借来的奇物。
t_en: Right. It was originally a Curio that my department had borrowed.

t_ch: 你见过它？
t_en: You've seen it?

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 废话，以前天天在我们科室里摆着呢，喏，现在你面前的这玩意儿就是。
t_en: I used to see it in our department every day. There, the thing in front of you is the Curio.

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 我们科用它和纳米膜做重力实验，按照原有计划，它受力就会「升维消失」，然后又「降维出现」。
t_en: My department used to do gravity experiments on it using nano membranes. The original plan was for it to "disappear by being elevated to a higher dimensional world" when it experiences gravity, then "re-appear back into our lower dimensional world."

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 但不知道是哪个冒失鬼调高了重力，搞得这玩意跟「瞬移」似的，现在在空间站里乱窜。
t_en: But some careless fool increased the gravitational force in the experiment, and this thing is currently teleporting all over the space station.

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 为了找这东西，我们科室全体出动了，可惜我们始终跟不上它的速度…还好，这次被我找到了。
t_en: My whole department is now out on the hunt for this thing, but we can never catch up to it... Luckily, I've found it for now.

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 好了不说了，我先记录下它的数据，再加个定位，免得待会儿又找不到了。
t_en: Well, I should record its data and install a tracking device to make sure I don't lose it again.

t_ch: （这东西竟然是件奇物，赶紧记下来回去告诉阿德勒吧。）
t_en: (So, this is actually a Curio. Let's note this down and tell Adler.)

t_ch: 辛苦辛苦，看你的样子，应该是有所收获了吧？
t_en: You've worked hard. Looks like you have some results?

t_ch: （将知道的事情告诉他）
t_en: (Tell him what you found.)

t_ch: 哇哦，所以说居然是一次奇物实验的产物？可惜无缘一见…等等。
t_en: Woah, so it's actually the result of a Curio experiment? Pity I didn't get to see it... Hold on.

t_ch: 「实验品十三号」…这名字好熟悉…之前我在我们科室里看到过诶？也就是说，我…我错过了？！
t_en: Experiment <unbreak>13</unbreak>... That name is so familiar... I think I've seen it in my department? In other words, I... I am missing out???

t_ch: 怎么这样啊，他们做实验居然不喊我，呜呜……
t_en: This is so mean! They are doing experiments without letting me know... *sob*

t_ch: 听说你又有新的怪谈了…
t_en: I heard you have new ghost stories...

t_ch: 就是那个没错！
t_en: That's the one!

t_ch: 效果拔群！
t_en: It's so effective!

t_ch: 该怎么做才好…
t_en: How should I do this...

t_ch: 我，即新星！
t_en: I am the rising star!

s_en: Busy Member of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 对了，你是哪位？也是来找「这个奇物」的吗？
t_en: By the way, who are you? Are you here for this Curio too?

t_ch: 「大大的期待！」嗯，没错，暗号就是这个。
t_en: "A big expectation!" Yes, this is the passphrase.

t_ch: 虽然要写的报告已经堆积如山，但是我还是没有停止搜寻新怪谈的脚步！
t_en: Although I have a pile of reports to write, I have not stopped in my journey of searching for new ghost stories!

t_ch: 不过这应该是我能研究的最后一个怪谈了。哎，温世玲那边催得紧……
t_en: But this is probably the last ghost story I can investigate. *sigh* Wen Shiling has really been on my tail...

t_ch: 这次的怪谈比较温馨，我觉得你应该会喜欢。在讲之前我先问一句：你有没有在空间站捡到过一些神秘的纸页？
t_en: The ghost story this time is pretty wholesome. I think you'll like it. Before that, let me ask: Have you ever picked up mysterious paper slips in the space station?

t_ch: 好像有…
t_en: I think so...

t_ch: 哇真的吗，那你得仔细听听，看看你捡到的是不是怪谈里「幸运签」哦！
t_en: Really? Then you need to listen carefully, and see if you've picked up the fortune leaf mentioned in the ghost story!

t_ch: 肯定没有！
t_en: Never!

t_ch: 真的吗？不过，我听呜呜伯们说，你好像很喜欢到处捡东西……
t_en: Really? But I heard from the Wubbaboos that you like to pick stuff up from everywhere...

t_ch: 最近有一个传闻，说地上会出现神秘纸页，叫做「幸运签」。捡起「幸运签」的人，会收获至高无上的好运。
t_en: There's been a rumor lately that mysterious pieces of paper appear on the ground sometimes. These are called fortune leaves, and people who pick them up are said to receive extremely good luck.

t_ch: 至于如何判断纸页是不是「幸运签」，很简单，在捡起「幸运签」时你会听到掌声，传闻这是星神对你的青睐与鼓励……
t_en: It's easy to determine whether a piece of paper is a fortune leaf — you will hear a round of applause when you pick one up. It is said to be the way Aeons express their admiration and encouragement for you...

t_ch: 目前已经有人说自己听到过，没过多久这人就晋升为某个科室的技术骨干！！
t_en: Someone had claimed to have heard it, and soon got promoted to technical leader in their department!

t_ch: 不过我还没有打听到是哪个科的…麻烦你就帮我去找到这个晋升的家伙，看看能不能从他嘴里挖出点有用的情报吧！
t_en: But I haven't found out which department this story is from... Could you please find this promoted person and see what useful information you can get out of them?

t_ch: 我的科研生涯怎么会变成这样啊…空间站是不是只有我还混得这么差啊？
t_en: How did my research career end up like this... Am I the only one in the space station having such a bad time?

t_ch: 你有捡到过幸运签吗？
t_en: Have you ever picked up a fortune leaf?

t_ch: 你看我的样子和「幸运」两个字有啥关系啊？
t_en: Do I look like I have anything to do with being lucky?

t_ch: 你没事吧？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 哎，你看我这样子就知道了——全身上下写满了「非常不幸」四个字。
t_en: Oh, just look at me. I've got "unlucky" written all over me.

t_ch: 哎，怎么我这么倒霉啊，工作不顺，家庭还闹矛盾……
t_en: *sigh* Why am I so unlucky? My career isn't going well, and my family is having arguments...

t_ch: 我听说就在这附近有个家伙捡了个什么玩意？总之就是能带来好运的东西，从此走向人生巅峰。
t_en: I heard someone picked something up around here? Something that can bring good luck? They got some big breaks after picking it up.

t_ch: 我就想着可以问问他在哪儿找到的，能不能分我一个半个的…哎，看来你也是同道之人啊。
t_en: I wanted to ask where they got that thing from, and if they can give even a bit of the luck to me... Haha, seems like you're also looking for this thing.

t_ch: 奇怪了，他们人呢？怎么还没来？
t_en: Weird. Where are they? Why are they still not here?

t_ch: 你也在找幸运签吗？
t_en: Are you also looking for the fortune leaf?

t_ch: 「幸运签」是什么？
t_en: What is the fortune leaf?

t_ch: 你在等人？
t_en: Are you waiting for someone?

t_ch: 对啊，我都等了好久了。
t_en: Yes. I've been waiting forever.

t_ch: 呃？我在等我们科室的人来开会，可你…看着不像我们科室的人呀，你是不是认错人了？
t_en: Huh? I'm waiting for people from my department to turn up for a meeting. But you... You don't look like you're from my department. Did you find the wrong person?

t_ch: （看来不是这个人，再找找看吧。）
t_en: (Doesn't seem to be the right person. Let's keep looking.)

t_ch: 不知道，不清楚，别问我。
t_en: I don't know. I don't have it. Don't ask me.

t_ch: 你很可疑。
t_en: You're pretty suspicious.

t_ch: 我还没问呢！
t_en: I haven't asked anything.

t_ch: 我不是，我没有，不知道！
t_en: Not me. I didn't do it. I don't know!

t_ch: 就是你拿到了幸运签吧？
t_en: So, you're the one with the fortune leaf.

t_ch: 听说你走向人生巅峰了？
t_en: I heard you got a big break in your career.

t_ch: …是又如何，不是又如何。年轻人还是要脚踏实地，别整天想着投机取巧。
t_en: ...So what if I did? And what if I didn't? Youngsters need to be grounded and stop thinking about taking shortcuts.

t_ch: 「幸运签」这种东西…呵呵，佩佩都不会信！
t_en: Fortune leaf... Heh, even Peppy won't believe that!

t_ch: 年轻就是要奋斗！
t_en: Youth is all about working hard!

t_ch: 我最恨投机取巧！
t_en: I detest those who take shortcuts!

t_ch: 呵，没想到你还挺有志气！
t_en: Hah, I didn't take you to be so spirited!

t_ch: 最近很多人来问我「幸运签」的事，他们总以为，捡起来两团纸就能从此一飞冲天。
t_en: A lot people have been asking me about the fortune leaf recently. They all think that just picking up a piece of paper would take their career to places.

t_ch: 但是，他们根本不知道这需要付出什么样的代价！你看我这个老胳膊老腿的…哎，不说了，不说了！
t_en: But they have no idea of the prices they must pay! Just look at my old body... Ahhh, never mind. Never mind!

t_ch: 我可以肯定地告诉你：年轻人，「幸运签」带你的好运，绝不是你们期待的那样！还是踏踏实实做人吧！
t_en: I will tell you, youngster, that the good fortunes brought by the fortune leaf are nothing like what you are expecting! Just stick to taking one step at a time!

t_ch: （总感觉另有隐情，但是也问不出更多的了。回去告诉阿德勒吧。）
t_en: (I feel there's something deeper here, but I can't get any more answers. Let's return to Adler.)

t_ch: 回来啦！我刚好打算休息休息呢…话说你都查到了什么？
t_en: You're back! I was planning to have a break... Did you find anything?

t_ch: （将见闻告知阿德勒）
t_en: (Tell Adler what you found.)

t_ch: 啊？就这？没啦？
t_en: Huh? That's it? Nothing more?

t_ch: 哎，要是只有这些线索和信息的话，我可没有办法写出研究报告， 那样的话好不容易积累的粉丝就要消失了……
t_en: *sigh* If this is the only information, then I can't manage to produce the report. Then I'm gonna lose all the fans I've worked so hard for...

t_ch: 说好的跨学科研究呢？
t_en: Weren't you doing this as cross-disciplinary research?

t_ch: 原来是为了涨粉啊…
t_en: So, it was all about getting fans...

t_ch: 好吧，我还是不狡辩了。我承认，最开始做这个研究是真正为了科学！但是后来我发现，很多人都爱看我写的「怪谈笔记」，我就被虚荣心打败了……
t_en: Okay, fine, I admit! Yes, initially this was about science! But then I realized so many people loved reading my ghost story compilation, and I yielded to my desire for attention...

t_ch: 你的研究初心变了！
t_en: You've lost the purpose behind your research!

t_ch: 我之后会好好反省的……
t_en: I will reflect on my actions...

t_ch: 那我有没有署名呢？
t_en: So, am I included as a co-author?

t_ch: 我会在助手那一栏里补上的！
t_en: I'll add you in as an assistant!

t_ch: 哎，我现在觉得，怪谈这种东西，都是标题党噱头多。
t_en: *sigh* Now I feel ghost stories only get attention when they have a click-bait title.

t_ch: 再有趣的怪谈在调查清楚之后，都变得相当无趣，这研究报告…不写也罢！
t_en: No matter how interesting a ghost story is, they stop being interesting once you've investigated them... I might as well stop writing this report!

t_ch: …呃，不写又挺可惜的。算了，就这样吧谢谢你这么久以来一直帮我哦！
t_en: ...Well, it'd be a pity not to write anymore. Anyway, thanks for having helped me for so long!

t_ch: 你还在连载怪谈笔记？
t_en: Are you still serializing your ghost stories?

t_ch: 想要重启人生…
t_en: I want to reset my life...

t_ch: 他们人呢？
t_en: Where are they?

t_ch: …和我无关。
t_en: ...Got nothing to do with me.

t_ch: 你过几天记得来找我，这次调查怎么说也有你的功劳，过来拿一份研究报告，就当纪念吧。
t_en: Come back to find me in a few days. Since you've contributed to the investigation, you should get a copy of the investigation report as a memento of your effort.

t_ch: 听说你有事找我？
t_en: I heard you are looking for me?

t_ch: 过几天我要和老姐妹们聚会，这么多年没见，我担心我这样会被她们笑话。
t_en: I'm catching up with some old girlfriends in a few days. It's been years since we last met up. I wonder... would they laugh at me for being out of touch with fashion?

t_ch: 我听艾丝妲站长提起过你，你见多识广，想必在时尚一道上也有独到见解。
t_en: I've heard of you from Lead Researcher Asta. You seem to know a lot, and might have your own insights on fashion.

t_ch: 我正好想请教请教你…你看我这头发，是不是该换个颜色了？
t_en: Can I ask for your advice? ...Do you think I should dye my hair a different color?

t_ch: 现在的就挺好。
t_en: The current color is fine.

t_ch: 但我觉着太老气了。
t_en: But I think it feels a little old-fashioned.

t_ch: 你想染什么颜色？
t_en: What color would you like?

t_ch: 哎，我也拿不准……
t_en: *sigh* I can't really decide...

t_ch: 时尚的车轮滚滚向前…我这头白发留了这么久，也换个颜色了。
t_en: Fashion is changing every day... I've had white hair for so long that it's about time for a change.

t_ch: 听说现在最流行的发色是蓝色和棕色，可两种发色都各有亮点，实在太难选了。
t_en: Apparently, the most fashionable colors these days are blue and brown, but both colors have their pros and cons. It's so hard to choose.

t_ch: 小孩子才做选择…
t_en: Why have to choose?

t_ch: 我倒是想全都要，但我这发质可经不起这么折腾咯。
t_en: I do want to try them both, but the quality of my hair won't allow me to experiment too much.

t_ch: 蓝色好，我喜欢蓝色！
t_en: Blue! I like blue!

t_ch: 蓝色让人联想到大海、星空、宇宙…诶打住打住！不是想问你喜欢哪个，我是想知道哪个发色更好看！
t_en: Blue makes people think about the sea, the sky, and the universe... Wait! I'm not asking which color you like. I want to know which hair color looks better!

t_ch: 棕色好，我喜欢棕色！
t_en: Brown! I like brown!

t_ch: 棕色有一种温暖的感觉…诶打住打住！不是想问你喜欢哪个，我是想知道哪个发色更好看！
t_en: Brown has a warm feeling to it... Wait! I'm not asking which color you like. I want to know which hair color looks better!

t_ch: 听说已经有科员染了这两种发色，我真想亲眼瞧瞧。
t_en: I heard some researchers have already dyed their hair these colors. I'd love to see them for myself.

t_ch: 可惜我还有密卷科的工作没做完，实在走不开…所以，能不能麻烦你帮我拍张照？
t_en: But my work at the Department of Insight is not done. I really can't leave yet... So, could I please ask you to take some photos for me?

t_ch: 我想看看，在空间站的灯光下这两种发色是什么效果，哪种发色会更适合我…唔，麻烦你帮个忙吧。
t_en: I want to see what these colors look like under the space station's lights, and which color would suit me better... Hmm, could you please lend a hand?

t_ch: （得找到发色是<color=#dbc291ff>蓝色</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>棕色</color>的人。）
t_en: (I need to find people with <color=#dbc291ff>blue</color> hair and <color=#dbc291ff>brown</color> hair.)

t_ch: （这头发的颜色不对吧，以斯帖想参考的是<color=#dbc291ff>蓝色</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>棕色</color>。）
t_en: (This isn't the right hair color. Esther wanted references for <color=#dbc291ff>blue</color> hair and <color=#dbc291ff>brown</color> hair.)

t_ch: （以斯帖要的照片都拍好了，带过去给她看看吧。）
t_en: (I'm done with Esther's photos. Time to take these back to her.)

t_ch: （将拍好的照片交给她）
t_en: (Give her the photos.)

t_ch: 乖乖，我看看啊…这效果不错，但我现在有个问题。
t_en: Ohhh, lemme see... This looks good. But I have an issue.

t_ch: 你看，在空间站的灯光下，蓝色好像有点死板，棕色又显得有点沉闷。
t_en: Look, the lights in the space station make blue look rigid, and make brown look dull.

t_ch: 怎么办啊，难道两个都不好看…{NICKNAME}，你是年轻人，你帮我看看哪个颜色更适合我？
t_en: What should I do? Maybe neither color looks good? {NICKNAME}, you're young and fashionable. Help me pick the color that suits me.

t_ch: 蓝色！
t_en: Blue!

t_ch: 就蓝色吧，确实不错，有一种宽广无垠的感觉。
t_en: Okay, blue it is. It's not bad. It gives a feeling of vastness and open space.

t_ch: 棕色！
t_en: Brown!

t_ch: 嗯，棕色是绝对不会出错的颜色。
t_en: Mmm, you can't go wrong with brown.

t_ch: 年轻人的脑子转速就是快，那我就听你的！这次的事情谢谢你啦！你也可以考虑换个发色试试哦。
t_en: Young people do think fast. I'll defer to you on this! Thank you. You can try a different hair color yourself, you know.

t_ch: 听说你又有事找我…
t_en: I heard you are looking for me again?

t_ch: 乖乖你来了，上次那个染头发的事真的很抱歉……
t_en: Ohhh, there you are. I'm so sorry about that business with my hair last time...

t_ch: 我没染成，总之浪费了你的一番心血，实在是不好意思。
t_en: I didn't end up dyeing my hair. I'm so sorry for wasting your efforts.

t_ch: 是不喜欢那个颜色吗？
t_en: You didn't like the color after all?

t_ch: 是出什么事了吗？
t_en: Did something happen?

t_ch: 那倒不是，只是我买了染发剂后，发现色差很大，但聚会在即，重新买又来不及……
t_en: No... I bought the dye, but the color was different from what I imagined. The party was coming up, and I had no time to buy another one...

t_ch: 我怕老姐妹们会笑话我，想了想还是决定不染了。
t_en: I was concerned my girlfriends would laugh at me, so I decided not to dye it after all.

t_ch: 那你打算怎么办？
t_en: What are your plans then?

t_ch: 哎，头发就这样了吧。
t_en: *sigh* I'll leave my hair as it is.

t_ch: 你现在这样也挺好。
t_en: You already look good.

t_ch: 乖乖，你不用夸我了，我这一头白发有什么好的。
t_en: Ohhh, there's no need to praise me. What's so good about white hair from old age?

t_ch: 说到聚会的事我就头疼…哎，既然头发是染不成了，那眼镜总可以换换吧。
t_en: I get a headache every time I think about the party... *sigh* If I can't dye my hair, then maybe I can at least change my glasses.

t_ch: 我看有不少科员都换上隐形眼镜了，我也想买来试试，就是不知道选什么款式好。
t_en: I see lots of researchers wearing contact lenses these days. I want to try them out, but I don't know which type to try.

t_ch: 哎，我还是有事走不开…你能帮我打听一下，现在流行什么款式的隐形眼镜吗？
t_en: *sigh* I'm still too busy to get away... Could you please ask around to see what contact lenses are in fashion these days?

t_ch: 呃，你有什么事吗？
t_en: Um, what do you want?

t_ch: 现在流行哪款隐形眼镜？
t_en: Which type of contact lenses is in fashion these days?

t_ch: 呃，我、我没用过诶，我也不太清楚。
t_en: Um, I—I'm not sure. I've never used those.

t_ch: 还是老老实实用框架眼镜吧，框架的就挺好啊，戴着还有一种书卷气。
t_en: Just use normal glasses with frames. They're pretty good. You even look sophisticated in them.

t_ch: 但平时总归有些不太方便吧？
t_en: But it still gets inconvenient once in a while, right?

t_ch: 我感觉至少不会伤眼睛嘛。
t_en: At least it won't hurt your eyes this way.

t_ch: 那也是，像我的话用了肯定两眼通红。
t_en: True. I would definitely get inflamed red eyes with contact lenses.

t_ch: 请问你要找谁？
t_en: Who are you looking for?

t_ch: 还是说…你有什么事吗？
t_en: Is there... anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 现在流行哪款隐形眼镜？
t_en: Which type of contact lenses is in fashion these days?

t_ch: 带有光学感应功能的？之前好像流行过一段时间。
t_en: The ones with optical sensing capabilities? Those were in fashion for a while.

t_ch: 不不不，在我们空间站里，当然是「天才俱乐部联名致敬款」。
t_en: No no no. In our space station, it's gotta be the Joint Tribute to the Genius Society lenses.

t_ch: …听起来怪怪的。
t_en: ...That sounds weird.

t_ch: 那是什么？
t_en: What is that?

t_ch: 简单来说就是印有黑塔女士指定图案的款式，我记得还挺抢手的。
t_en: Basically, it's a lens with the picture of Madam Herta imprinted on it. I remember it was very popular.

t_ch: 那是大半年前的款式…现在还流不流行都要打个问号了。
t_en: That was from more than six months ago... I'm not sure if it's still in fashion.

t_ch: 而且我记得…这款早就售罄了，有钱都买不到。我说你呀，还是别瞎指导人了。
t_en: Also, I'm pretty sure that type sold out ages ago. You can't even buy it with money now. Don't mislead other people.

t_ch: 呃，那现在流行的是什么我也不清楚…抱歉，抱歉。
t_en: Umm, okay. I'm not sure what's fashionable anymore... Sorry...

t_ch: 你好…请问你是？
t_en: Hi... And you are?

t_ch: 现在流行哪款隐形眼镜？
t_en: Which type of contact lenses is in fashion these days?

t_ch: 哈哈，你真有眼光，这可算是问对人了。你看我现在戴着的就是时下最流行的爆款！
t_en: Haha, you've got good taste! I'm the right person to ask that question. See, the ones I'm wearing right now are the hottest and most popular lenses!

t_ch: 「琥珀星光」——琥珀色底色，带有自定义变色星光纹路，关了灯特别酷炫，在人群里就是最亮的崽！
t_en: Amber Starlight — With an amber-hued background and customizable, color-changing starlight patterns, it looks super cool when the lights are off. You'll be the coolest person all around!

t_ch: 果真吗，你不要骗我。
t_en: Really? Don't troll me.

t_ch: 我可是空间站里响当当的「潮人」！
t_en: Hey! I'm the bona fide fashion guru of the entire space station!

t_ch: 还有别的款式推荐吗？
t_en: Do you have any other recommendations?

t_ch: 没了，当季最流行的就是这款，没有之一！
t_en: Nope. This is the most popular style this season. No other options.

t_ch: 哎，你别看我的样子平平无奇，但我姐姐可是个设计师，耳濡目染得多了，我敢说空间站没人比我更懂潮流！
t_en: C'mon, I may look plain, but my older sister is a designer. After having watched her for so long, I'm willing to bet that no one on the space station understands fashion more than me!

t_ch: （总算问到了，赶紧把这个好消息告诉以斯帖。）
t_en: (I finally got an answer. I should hurry and tell Esther the good news.)

t_ch: 现在流行「琥珀星光」。
t_en: Amber Starlight is in fashion right now.

t_ch: 稍等，我查一下…喔，确实好看！但宣传页面上的那个效果，感觉很难实现啊。
t_en: Hold on, let me check... Oh, it does look good! But I feel it'll be difficult to re-create what they're showing on the ad.

t_ch: …但如果不买，我就只能戴着这副框架眼镜去聚会了，这样又太老气了。
t_en: ...However, if I don't buy it, I'll have to wear this pair of wired glasses to the party. That's too old-fashioned.

t_ch: {NICKNAME} ，你觉得我该选哪个？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what should I do?

t_ch: 就戴框架眼镜吧。
t_en: Stick with wired glasses.

t_ch: 嗯，「琥珀星光」看起来太花里胡哨了，我这么大岁数了，的确不合适。
t_en: Hmm, yes. Amber Starlight is too showy. It doesn't really fit me in my age.

t_ch: 当然是买「琥珀星光」！
t_en: Definitely Amber Starlight!

t_ch: 虽然特效有些夸张，但琥珀色真的很好看，我也很心动！
t_en: The special effects are rather extravagant, but the amber shade is quite pretty. I am definitely tempted!

t_ch: 谢谢你啦，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 听说你又有事找我…
t_en: I heard you are looking for me again?

t_ch: 乖乖，多亏了你啊，去聚会的时候老姐妹们都夸我，说我现在是个「潮人」了。
t_en: I have to thank you. My girlfriends at the party all said that I'm a fashionista now.

t_ch: 她们都很羡慕我能有你这么一个时尚顾问。这次她们拜托我，说是有些时尚方面的问题想请教你——
t_en: They're really jealous that I have a fashion consultant like you. This time, they want me to ask you some questions...

t_ch: 最近很流行的「复古鎏金古铜色」和「夏日冷萃丛林绿」两种颜色，{NICKNAME}，你有什么头绪吗？
t_en: Two colors are really popular right now. Retro Glazed Bronze and Summer Blooming Forest... {NICKNAME}, do you know what they actually are?

t_ch: 不就是金色和绿色…
t_en: It's just gold and green...

t_ch: 乖乖，我就说你时尚触觉敏锐吧…这么冷门的颜色你都能秒懂。
t_en: Wow, I knew it. Your fashion instincts are sharp... You immediately understood those weird names.

t_ch: 空间站里很常见啊！
t_en: Those colors are everywhere in the space station!

t_ch: 真的吗？我怎么没注意到呢？
t_en: Really? I've never noticed it.

t_ch: 照你这么说，这两种色系…空间站里好像就有，但我完全没印象呢。
t_en: So you're saying the space station already... has these two shades? But I don't remember ever seeing them.

t_ch: 这样吧，拜托你帮我把符合这种色系的东西拍下来，然后带给我瞅瞅，让我也长长眼！
t_en: In that case, please help me take pictures of items in these colors and bring the photos to me, so I can learn about them as well!

t_ch: （金色和绿色还挺常见的，空间站里的<color=#dbc291ff>植物花卉</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>星穹列车</color>都对得上号……）
t_en: (Gold and green should be quite common. The <color=#dbc291ff>plants and flowers</color> in the space station and the <color=#dbc291ff>Astral Express</color> should be enough...)

t_ch: （这个颜色吧，以斯帖想找的是<color=#dbc291ff>金色</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>绿色</color>。）
t_en: (This color looks fine... Esther wanted <color=#dbc291ff>gold</color> and <color=#dbc291ff>green</color>.)

t_ch: （以斯帖要的照片都拍好了，带过去给她看看吧。）
t_en: (I'm done with Esther's photos. Time to take these back to her.)

t_ch: （将拍好的照片给她看）
t_en: (Show her the photos.)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你是年轻人，脑子转数和行动力可真强。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're definitely a youngster. You think fast and act fast.

t_ch: 但我真的没想到啊，「复古鎏金古铜色」和「夏日冷萃丛林绿」看名字挺新奇的。
t_en: But I really didn't expect this. Retro Glazed Bronze and Summer Blooming Forest... they sound so new and special based on the name.

t_ch: 真是开了眼了，居然就是这么常见的颜色，我总感觉这是否有点……
t_en: I really learned something today. They're just ordinary colors. I feel maybe this is a bit...

t_ch: 潮流就是个噱头！
t_en: Fashion is not real!

t_ch: 以前总听人说，所谓的潮流，其实就是个噱头，看来这话说得挺对。
t_en: I've always heard people say that fashion is just a trick, that it's not real. Looks like they're right.

t_ch: 有点名过于实？
t_en: Over-hyped?

t_ch: 这么普通的颜色…的确有点名过于实。
t_en: With such ordinary colors... it's definitely over-hyped.

t_ch: 哎，你说得对。不过我现在感觉…一时半会儿还有点消化不了。
t_en: You're right. But I feel... I can't digest all this information at once.

t_ch: 在我年轻的时候，也流行过这种撞色搭配，没想到现在又流行起来了。
t_en: This kind of contrast was fashionable when I was young. I didn't think it'd be back in fashion now.

t_ch: 时尚这玩意真奇怪，就像个轮回…啧啧！
t_en: Fashion is so weird. It's all a big cycle... Tsk!

t_ch: 听说你需要帮助。
t_en: I hear you need help.

t_ch: 啊，我确实有件事想找人帮忙来着……
t_en: Ahh, I do have something I want help with...

t_ch: 我之前委托过几个关系不错的银鬃铁卫，让他们在出外勤的时候顺便帮我留意野生动物的动向。
t_en: Previously, I asked some Silvermane Guards I knew to help me look for traces of wild animals when they go out on patrol.

t_ch: 很长一段时间过去了，我一点消息都没收到…直到最近。
t_en: It has been a long time since then, and I haven't received any news from them... until now.

t_ch: 前几天，有个铁卫兴冲冲地给告诉我，他在南边的雪原目击到了冰原狼的活动——这可是件大事啊！
t_en: An excited Guard told me a few days ago that he saw activities of direwolves on the Southern Snow Plains — That's big news!

t_ch: 但我实在不好意思劳烦铁卫帮我收集野生动物留下的痕迹了…毕竟他们的本职工作已经够辛苦了。
t_en: But I really don't want to keep asking the Guards to help me collect traces of wild animals... Their own job is difficult enough as it is.

t_ch: 我能看出来，你的探险经历很丰富，对吧？如果可以的话…我能委托你去城郊雪原寻找野生动物留下的痕迹吗？
t_en: I can tell that you have a lot of experience adventuring, right? If possible... Could I ask you to head to the outlying Snow Plains and find traces of wild animals?

t_ch: 皮毛、脚印、甚至粪便都可以！拜托啦，我肯定会准备丰厚的谢礼报答你的。
t_en: Fur, paw marks, and even excrement are okay! Please! I will definitely repay you with rich rewards!

t_ch: 这是调查结果。
t_en: There are the findings.

t_ch: 这些…这些的确是冰原狼活动过的证据，太棒了！有了这些线索，说不定很快就能追踪到贝洛伯格附近的野狼族群了。
t_en: These... these are indeed evidence of direwolf activity. Awesome! With these clues, I might be able to find the direwolf packs close to Belobog.

t_ch: 这可真是了不起的发现！有生之年，真想能亲眼欣赏一下那些美丽又野性的野生动物啊……
t_en: This is an outstanding find! Oh, I really want to look at those beautiful and majestic wild animals with my own eyes...

t_ch: 没有野生动物的世界实在无趣。
t_en: A world without wild animals would be so boring.

t_ch: 对吧？我也这么觉得。
t_en: Right? I think so too.

t_ch: 万一它们真的灭绝了…
t_en: What if they already went extinct...

t_ch: 我…我实在不想相信那种事。
t_en: I... I really don't want to imagine that.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格只是这个世界的一隅。我相信在其它角落，一定还有其它生命在努力地生存，与寒潮作斗争。
t_en: Belobog is only one location in this world. I believe there must be other lifeforms elsewhere combating the Eternal Freeze and trying their best to survive.

t_ch: 我真心希望人类在这个世界上不是孤独的存在…所以我想找到其它物种，想办法保护它们，或是与它们和谐共存。
t_en: I truly hope humans are not the sole existence in this world... That's why I want to find other species, and to try to protect them or live with them.

t_ch: 这次真的辛苦你了——请收下这些报酬吧！要是旅行途中发现了任何珍稀动物，一定记得转告我哦。
t_en: Thank you so much for this — Please take these rewards! If you find any rare animals on your journeys, please let me know.

t_ch: 雪原上留下了和狗相近的脚印，一直向前，几乎没有改变方向或者步伐。数量上应该是两到三只。
t_en: There are footprints in the snow, similar to that of a dog. The footprints continue forward without changing direction. From the looks of it, there are two or three of them.

t_ch: 雪地上留下了白色的鬃毛，在附近的树丛里还发现了咬痕。
t_en: There is some white fur left in the snow. And some bite marks are found in the bushes nearby.

t_ch: …是粪便。乍一看，其中包含了动物毛发、骨头碎片、以及果实残块。是杂食动物。
t_en: ...Feces. At first glance, it contains animal fur, bone fragments, and digested fruits. The animal must be an omnivore.

t_ch: 听说你有事求人？
t_en: I heard you need help?

t_ch: 哦，你居然是为了这个来的？实在是件小事，不足挂齿……
t_en: Oh, you came all the way just for this? It's but a trivial matter.

t_ch: 我向来乐于助人。
t_en: I always like to help other people.

t_ch: …你当真想帮我这个忙？
t_en: You really want to help me?

t_ch: 有话直说！
t_en: Just tell me!

t_ch: …好说好说，你消消气。
t_en: ...Okay, okay. Calm down.

t_ch: 那我就直说了。你也知道，人活到我这个程度，简单的物质已经很难刺激神经，生活的空虚必须依靠其它方式填满。
t_en: Well, then I'll just get to the point. As you know, when you're well-off to a certain degree, simple material possessions aren't as stimulating anymore. I need to fill the void in my life by other means.

t_ch: 我从很久以前开始就一直在追问自己，人生到底还有什么是值得追求的？就在不久之前，我独自冥想的时候…答案突然在脑海中显现了。
t_en: What is there in life that is worth pursuing? For a very long time, I've been asking myself this question. And then one day, the answer came to me when I was meditating alone.

t_ch: 没错，我要找的东西就是「朋友」——我，华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，这一生中从来没拥有过真心的朋友。这一点必须改变！
t_en: That's right. The thing I am looking for is a friend. I, Wallace Cambridge Limestein, have never had a true friend in my life. And that must be changed!

t_ch: 不管花多少钱都行，我一定要得到「朋友」这样东西。
t_en: I would pay anything to acquire this thing they call a "friend."

t_ch: 「朋友」不是东西……
t_en: A "friend" isn't a thing...

t_ch: 嗯，有点道理…如果「朋友」是东西的话，那我直接用钱买就行了。
t_en: Well, that makes sense... If "friends" are things, then I can just buy them with money.

t_ch: 钱买不到真心的「朋友」。
t_en: Money can't buy true friends.

t_ch: 「真心」？加点钱能买到吗？
t_en: "True"? What if I pay extra?

t_ch: …多余的话就不说了，还是来聊聊怎么帮我交到朋友吧。
t_en: I'm not going to keep talking. We'd better discuss how I can make friends.

t_ch: 我权衡了一下，以我的身份和格局，跑到大街上逮人就问未免有些自降身价。于是我想到一个精妙的点子：拍照。
t_en: I've thought about it. With my reputation, I will be degrading myself if I just ask anyone on the street to be my friend. So I have a great idea: Photos.

t_ch: 拍照？
t_en: Photos?

t_ch: 没错，拍照。
t_en: Yes, photos.

t_ch: 以摄影会友，是个好点子。
t_en: Making friends with photography as a hobby is a great idea.

t_ch: …你可能误会了，这不是我的本意。
t_en: It seems you misunderstood. That's not what I meant.

t_ch: 通过他人之手，将华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀最富魅力的时刻记录下来，让路人为之倾倒，甘愿成为我的朋友——这就是我的策略。
t_en: My strategy is to capture my most glamorous moments in photos so that any passerby will be wowed and want to become my friends.

t_ch: 年轻人，请正面回答我，你的拍照技术如何？
t_en: {F#Young lady}{M#Young man}, please answer me honestly, how are your photography skills?

t_ch: 毫无经验。
t_en: Poor. I have no experience.

t_ch: 这样吗？可惜……
t_en: Oh, too bad...

t_ch: 不过无妨。以我自身的气质和镜头感，摄影师应该不需要太高的造诣就能捕捉到颇具风采的画面。
t_en: But it's okay. With my charisma and photogenic face, the photo will come out great even if the photographer is not an expert.

t_ch: 马马虎虎。
t_en: I'm not a pro, my skills are mediocre.

t_ch: 将就而已？嗯…问题倒也不大。
t_en: That will do.

t_ch: 以我自身的气质和镜头感，摄影师应该不需要太高的造诣就能捕捉到颇具风采的画面。
t_en: With my charisma and photogenic face, the photo will come out great even if the photographer is not an expert.

t_ch: 大师水准。
t_en: I am a pro.

t_ch: 哦？你还真是自信满满。
t_en: Oh? You're pretty confident.

t_ch: 如果真的如你所说，你的摄影技术加上我本人的气质和镜头感，一定能营造出足以俘获众生的摄影作品。
t_en: If you are as good as you say, then your photography skills coupled with my charisma and photogenic face will surely make photos that can captivate all sentient beings.

t_ch: 择日不如撞日，我们现在就开工。来，先以这永冬铭碑为背景，帮我拍一张照片吧。
t_en: There's no time like the present. Let's start now. Come on, take a picture of me with the Everwinter Monument as the backdrop.

t_ch: 拍完了？让我看看……
t_en: Done? Let's have a look...

t_ch: …唔，感觉今天皮肤的状态不是很好。下次拍照之前我要先把美容院包下来。
t_en: Hmm, looks like my skin isn't looking its best today. For the next photo shoot, I will first make myself photo-ready by using the whole worth of a beauty salon on me.

t_ch: 总体来说还算过得去，就这样吧。好了，年轻人——赶紧带上这张照片，去帮我找朋友吧。
t_en: This one is fine. Alright, {M#young man}{F#young lady}, take these pictures with you and help me make friends.

t_ch: …这也要我来？
t_en: I need to do that for you?

t_ch: 你怎么不自己去？
t_en: Why don't you do it yourself?

t_ch: …唔，刚才我也说了，以我的身份，满大街找人聊天实在不成体统。跑腿的事就麻烦你了…放心，我的家境你也不是不知道，报酬不会少了你的。
t_en: Hmm... Like I said, given my status, it's inappropriate for me to chat with strangers on the street. You know I am wealthy. If you help me with this, I will reward you handsomely.

t_ch: 哦，有关人选的问题，我觉得还是应该缩小一下范围，免得你找上些于我无益的闲杂人士。
t_en: Oh, regarding the candidates, I think it would be better to narrow the scope down before you bring in some randos who are of no use to me.

t_ch: 我听说政务部有个叫曼娅的公务员，出身很不错，和我家也有点渊源。你能否带着照片去帮我问问，看看她愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: I heard that there's an Administrative Official named Manya, who is of good lineage — in fact, our families have a bit of history. Could you show the photo to her and ask her if she wants to be my friend?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 什么，谁？你这人说话怎么没头没脑的，真奇怪……
t_en: What? Who? I don't understand what you are saying. You are strange...

t_ch: 我带了他的照片…
t_en: I brought a picture of him...

t_ch: 照片…？唔，虽然感觉有些诡异…但还是拿给我看看吧。
t_en: Picture...? Photo...? Hmm, it feels a little weird... but show it to me anyway.

t_ch: 他说你们两家有些渊源。
t_en: He said your families have some history.

t_ch: 渊源…？奇怪了，我怎么从来没听说过……
t_en: History...? Strange, I've never heard of him...

t_ch: 等等，这个人……
t_en: Wait, this man...

t_ch: 哎呀，我想起来了——这是莱姆士汀家的那位阔少吧？说是阔少，其实现在也已经一把年纪了……
t_en: Oh, I remember now. This is the son of the Limestein family, right? They say he is young and rich, but actually, he is quite old...

t_ch: 他怎么突然想起来找我了？不行，曼娅，你不能冲动，得仔细分析一下……
t_en: Why is he looking for me all of a sudden? No, wait! Don't be impulsive, Manya! You have to be careful!

t_ch: 莱姆士汀虽然富有，但本质上只是暴发户，并没有其他贵族的底蕴……
t_en: The Limesteins are wealthy, but they're also new money. They have no aristocratic roots...

t_ch: 华劳斯现年三十八岁又两个月，没有担任任何公职，传闻他整日游手好闲，莱姆士汀家族也不是很喜欢他……
t_en: Wallace is <unbreak>38</unbreak> years and 2 months old. He's never held a public office, and I hear he's a complete loafer. The Limestein family doesn't appear to like him much either...

t_ch: 你怎么这么了解…
t_en: How do you know so much about...

t_ch: 你还在犹豫什么？
t_en: Why are you hesitating?

t_ch: 短期来看，我似乎有机会一跃进入上流社会！但是莱家树敌无数，在政坛缺乏根基。一旦莱家失势，或者华劳斯被莱家抛弃……
t_en: In the short term, I'd be in with a chance of joining the upper classes! But the Limesteins have so many enemies, and no real foundation in political circles. If they were to lose their influence, or if Wallace were to be discarded by his family...

t_ch: …那我这个做朋友的说不定也会受到影响，仕途受挫！
t_en: ...Then I might also be affected, and my career will be ruined!

t_ch: 不行，我不能答应！麻烦你转告华劳斯，就说我倍感荣幸…但果然我们两家还是保持距离为好。
t_en: No, I can't be friends with him! Please tell Wallace that I'm flattered, but it's better for us to keep our distance.

t_ch: 曼娅不想和你做朋友…
t_en: Manya doesn't want to be your friend...

t_ch: …什么？她看过照片了吗？她有说什么吗？
t_en: What...? Has she seen the photo? Did she say anything?

t_ch: 她说…
t_en: She said...

t_ch: 将和曼娅的谈话内容转述给了华劳斯……
t_en: You convey what Manya said to Wallace...

t_ch: 这样啊…原来如此。听你这么一说，为了她自身的仕途考虑，曼娅女士确实有充分的理由不和我做朋友。
t_en: I see. Ms. Manya does have every reason not to be friends with me for the sake of her own career.

t_ch: 不过莱姆士汀家的人是不会轻易气馁的。我迟早会交到「真心」的朋友，让拒我于门外的人后悔。
t_en: But we Limesteins are not quitters. Sooner or later, I will find a "true" friend, and those who rejected me will regret it.

t_ch: 感谢你的帮助，年轻人。这是答应你的报酬——下次我再需要帮忙的时候，希望你还在。
t_en: Thank you for your help. This is the payment I promised. I hope you can still help me the next time I need your help.

t_ch: 听说你又在找朋友了？
t_en: I heard you're looking for friends again?

t_ch: 没错，年轻人。我，华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，想继续在这茫茫人海中寻找真心朋友。
t_en: Yes. I, Wallace Cambridge Limestein, wish to continue to seek true friends.

t_ch: 我总结了过去的失败，得出一个结论：错不在我。
t_en: I've studied my failures and come to one conclusion: It's not my fault.

t_ch: 我的心中没有任何杂念，只想交一个真心朋友。反倒是我接触的那些人顾忌太多，或是对我的家世怀有疑问，或是对我抱持着无中生有的偏见。
t_en: I have no other intentions. I just want to make true friends. It was the people I came into contact with... They were too cautious. Some of them had suspicions about my family background, and some held prejudices against me for no reason.

t_ch: 我相信，只要继续把持莱姆士汀家真诚的待人之道，本人有朝一日必定能友遍天下。
t_en: I believe that I will one day make friends all over the world as long as I continue to treat people with sincerity like all the Limesteins.

t_ch: 我欣赏你的自信。
t_en: I admire your confidence.

t_ch: 你对「真诚」的定义很特别…
t_en: Your understanding of "sincerity" is pretty unusual...

t_ch: 干巴巴的讨论永远比不上实际行动。走吧，我已经物色好了今天的拍照地点。
t_en: Meaningless discussion can never be compared to real action. Come on, I've found a photo spot for today.

t_ch: 摄影需要故事方能吸引人的眼球，而站在你眼前的恰巧是一个有故事的男人——你要做的就是把我散发魅力的瞬间捕捉下来。
t_en: A photo needs a story to attract attention, and you're looking at a man full of stories. All you have to do is capture the moment when I exude charm.

t_ch: 来吧，来行政广场的喷泉找我。该让你看到华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀不一样的一面了。
t_en: Come and meet me at the fountain in the Administrative District Plaza. It's time to show you a different side of me.

t_ch: 拍完了？让我看看……
t_en: Done? Let's have a look...

t_ch: 不错，不错…你抓住了精髓。我的目的就是要让人们看到，华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀并非不谙世事的阔少爷——相反，我很有亲和力，很受孩子们欢迎。
t_en: Good... This is a great photo. My purpose is to show people that I am not just some ignorant rich man. On the contrary, I am very friendly and popular with children.

t_ch: 好了，该去帮我找朋友了。和以往一样，要找的人由我来指定。
t_en: All right, it's time to help me make friends. Just like before, I will decide who I want to make friends with.

t_ch: 我听说波瓦大臣家的公子罗希是个性格豪放的年轻人，说不定和我能聊得来。你能否带着照片去帮我问问，看看他愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: I heard that Rossy, son of Minister Bova, is a young man with a bold personality. Maybe we are on the same wavelength. Could you show the photo to him and ask him if he's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 什么？莱姆士汀家的那个华劳斯？我没听错吧？
t_en: What? Wallace Limestein? Did I hear you right?

t_ch: 不行，绝无可能！那莱姆士汀家尽出些爱挥霍的纨绔子弟，我老爸早就警告过我，不能跟他们沾上关系！
t_en: No way! The Limesteins are dandies who are always reckless with money. My father warned me long ago not to get involved with them!

t_ch: 也许这张照片能让你改变看法？
t_en: Maybe this photo will change your mind?

t_ch: 照片？什么照片？给我看看……
t_en: Photo? What photo? Let me see...

t_ch: 这是什么？华劳斯？旁边还围着一群小孩…这不是他的小孩吧？
t_en: What's this? Is that Wallace? He's surrounded by a bunch of kids... These are not his children, are they?

t_ch: 哎哟，不行，我看见小孩就来气！你知道么，贝洛伯格三十岁往上的成年人每年近四成的收入都投入在了小孩身上——
t_en: Gosh, I can't, I can't stand the sight of children! Do you know that nearly forty percent of the annual income of adults over age thirty in Belobog is spent on children?

t_ch: ——怪不得当今社会成年人的精神健康状况普遍堪忧呢，拼死拼活赚的那几个子全都被这群吸金小鬼给吞进肚了！
t_en: No wonder the mental health of adults in today's society is poor. All the money we've worked so hard for has been invested in these kids!

t_ch: 赶紧把照片拿走，我看不得这玩意——那莱姆士汀是故意派你来气我的吧？你给我转告他，我罗希这辈子都不可能跟他交朋友！
t_en: Take this picture away. I don't like it. Wallace sent you here to grind my gears, didn't he? Tell him that I, Rossy, will never be his friend!

t_ch: 罗希不想和你做朋友…
t_en: Rossy doesn't want to be your friend...

t_ch: …什么？他看过照片了吗？他有说什么吗？
t_en: What...? Has he seen the photo? Did he say anything?

t_ch: 他说…
t_en: He said...

t_ch: 将和罗希的谈话内容转述给了华劳斯……
t_en: You convey what Rossy said to Wallace...

t_ch: 原来如此…原来那个罗希这么讨厌小孩子啊。
t_en: I see. It turns out that Rossy hates children.

t_ch: 本以为我俩同为名门出身，能有许多共同语言，但没想到他居然对莱姆士汀家保持着如此谬误的偏见。这个朋友，不交也罢。
t_en: I thought since we are both from notable households, we would have a lot in common, but I didn't expect him to have such a nasty prejudice against my family. I don't need friends like him.

t_ch: 不过莱姆士汀家的人是不会轻易气馁的。我迟早会交到「真心」的朋友，让拒我于门外的人后悔。
t_en: But we Limesteins are not quitters. Sooner or later, I will find a "true" friend, and those who rejected me will regret it.

t_ch: 感谢你的帮助，年轻人。这是你的报酬——下次我再需要帮忙的时候，希望你还在。
t_en: Thank you for your help. This is the payment I promised. I hope you can still help me the next time I need your help.

t_en: I heard you're looking for friends again?

t_en: Yes. I, Wallace Cambridge Limestein, wish to continue to seek true friends.

t_en: I've studied my failures and come to one conclusion: It's not my fault.

t_ch: 我欣赏你的自信。
t_en: I admire your confidence.

t_ch: 你对「真诚」的定义很特别…

t_en: Meaningless discussion can never be compared to real action. Come on, I've found a photo spot for today.

t_en: A photo needs a story to attract attention, and you're looking at a man full of stories. All you have to do is capture the moment when I exude charm.

t_ch: 来吧，来行政区的南边找我。该让你看到华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀不一样的一面了。
t_en: Come and meet me on the south side of the Administrative District. It's time to show you a different side of me.

t_ch: 拍完了？让我看看……
t_en: Done? Let's have a look...

t_ch: 不错，不错…你抓住了精髓。这辆车是我心爱的座驾之一，可能是世界上仅剩的一辆还能正常上街的<unbreak>650</unbreak>年限量款「铜墙」牌轿车。
t_en: Good... This is a great photo. This car is one of my all-time favorites, and probably the only <unbreak>650</unbreak>-year-old limited edition "Bronze Wall" sedan left in the world that can still drive normally.

t_ch: 要问它的状态为什么这么好，那都多亏了我数年如一日的不懈打理…当然，我雇佣的二十人机动车保养小组也有一定的功劳。
t_en: If you want to ask why it's in such good condition, it's all thanks to my years of care... Of course, the <unbreak>20</unbreak>-person car maintenance team I hired also had a hand in it.

t_ch: 好了，该去帮我找朋友了。和以往一样，要找的人由我来指定。
t_en: All right, it's time to help me make friends. Just like before, I will decide who I want to make friends with.

t_ch: 我听说有个名叫费尔多的法官在亚历珊德拉广场一带转悠——我一直希望能拓展自己在法律界的人脉。你能否带着照片去帮我问问，看看他愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: I heard there's a judge named Furdo at the Alexandra Plaza, and I've always wanted to expand my connections in the legal industry. Could you show the photo to him and ask him if he's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 华劳斯？哪位？是找我咨询过法律问题的市民吗？恕我直言，我每天要见那么多人，怎么可能每个名字都记住……
t_en: Wallace? Who is that? Is he a citizen who came to me for legal advice before? With all due respect, I need to see so many people every day... How could I remember each of their names?

t_ch: 他是莱姆士汀家的公子。
t_en: He's a Limestein.

t_ch: 莱姆士汀…哦，是那个有名的贵族世家？我好像隐约听说过那位公子哥的事迹。
t_en: Limestein... Oh, that famous noble family? I think I've heard quite a bit about his deeds.

t_ch: 所以呢？这和我有什么关系？
t_en: So? What's that got to do with me?

t_ch: 他很有钱…
t_en: He's rich...

t_ch: 呃…所以呢？这和我有什么关系？
t_en: Uh... so what? What does it have to do with me?

t_ch: 你看看这张照片。
t_en: Look at this photo.

t_ch: 照片？怎么又要看照片了？你这人做事不讲逻辑，我们学法的最忌讳这个……
t_en: Photo? Why should I look at this photo? You are a bit illogical. And acting without logic is the deadliest thing for us who study law.

t_ch: …这个人就是华劳斯？长相很普通嘛。倒是这车不错……
t_en: So, is that Wallace? He's just average-looking, but that car of his does seem pretty nice.

t_ch: 哦，我懂了！他是需要专业的法律咨询服务，对么？说吧，是汽车保险合同纠纷还是交通事故理赔？
t_en: Oh, I know what he wants from me! He needs professional legal advice, doesn't he? Tell me, is it about a car insurance contract dispute, or perhaps a traffic accident settlement?

t_ch: 他只是想和你交朋友…
t_en: He just wants to be friends with you...

t_ch: 你就不能盼人点好？
t_en: Can't you think of something less dreary?

t_ch: …意思是这里边没有法律纠纷，这位公子哥单纯地想和我交朋友？
t_en: So... there is no legal trouble, and this man just wants to be my friend?

t_ch: 奇怪…太奇怪了，简直可疑。我看不清楚这个华劳斯的动机…这可不是什么好兆头！
t_en: This is strange and suspicious. I can't understand Wallace's motives... That's not a good sign!

t_ch: 麻烦你转告这位…华劳斯，抱歉，我不能接受他的邀请。我们法律人必须洁身自好，绝对不能主动踏进灰色地带……
t_en: Please tell Wallace that I'm sorry, but I can't be his friend. As a judge, I must maintain a good reputation and should never step into any gray area...

t_ch: 费尔多不想和你做朋友…
t_en: Furdo doesn't want to be your friend...

t_ch: …什么？他看过照片了吗？他有说什么吗？
t_en: What...? Has he seen the photo? Did he say anything?

t_ch: 他说…
t_en: He said...

t_ch: 将和费尔多的谈话内容转述给了华劳斯……
t_en: You convey what Furdo said to Wallace...

t_ch: 原来如此…那位叫费尔多的法官很有职业素养，他拒绝的理由我完全理解。可惜了，我一直希望能和一身正气的人做朋友。
t_en: I see... Judge Furdo is very professional, and I completely understand why he refused to be friends with me. Too bad, I always wanted to be friends with righteous people.

t_ch: 不过莱姆士汀家的人是不会轻易气馁的。我迟早会交到「真心」的朋友，让拒我于门外的人后悔。
t_en: But we Limesteins are not quitters. Sooner or later, I will find a "true" friend, and those who rejected me will regret it.

t_ch: 感谢你的帮助，年轻人。这是你的报酬——下次我再需要帮忙的时候，希望你还在。
t_en: Thank you for your help. This is the payment I promised. I hope you can still help me the next time I need your help.

t_en: I heard you're looking for friends again?

t_en: A photo needs a story to attract attention, and you're looking at a man full of stories. All you have to do is capture the moment when I exude charm.

t_ch: 来吧，来黄金歌剧院的大门口找我。该让你看到华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀不一样的一面了。
t_en: Come and meet me at the gate of the Golden Theater. It's time to show you a different side of me.

t_ch: 拍完了？让我看看……
t_en: Done? Let's have a look...

t_ch: 不错，不错…你抓住了精髓。不瞒你说，我对音乐剧颇有研究，像《雪国往事》和《白衣之后》这类经典剧目，我都欣赏过不下二十遍。
t_en: Good... This is a great photo. As a matter of fact, I have done a lot of research on musicals. I have enjoyed classic dramas such as "Once Upon a Snowsieged Country" and "Beneath the White Cloak" more than twenty times.

t_ch: 要是我未来的朋友恰巧也对音乐剧感兴趣，那兴许我们可以一起包场观看——包场费由我担负。
t_en: If my future friend happens to be interested in musicals, maybe we can book a whole theater and watch a show together. I'll pay for all that, of course.

t_ch: 好了，该去帮我找朋友了。和以往一样，要找的人由我来指定。
t_en: All right, it's time to help me make friends. Just like before, I will decide who I want to make friends with.

t_ch: 我听说行政区有个有名的房地产中介，名叫拉雯妮。正巧，莱姆士汀家旗下拥有小几十套房产，她应该听过我的名字。
t_en: I heard there's a famous real estate agent named Lavonne in the Administrative District. Well, my family owns dozens of properties. She should have heard of me.

t_ch: 你能否带着照片去帮我问问，看看她愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: Could you show the photo to her and ask her if she's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 华劳斯？你是说莱姆士汀家的那个华劳斯？
t_en: Wallace? You mean Wallace Limestein?

t_ch: 好呀，我答应了。
t_en: Sure, I can be his friend.

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 你不按套路出牌…
t_en: I didn't expect you to say yes...

t_ch: …有什么好惊讶的？那可是莱姆士汀呀！你知道他们家在行政区有多少套房产吗？
t_en: Why are you surprised? He is a Limestein! Do you know how many properties his family owns in the Administrative District?

t_ch: 如果能和华劳斯少爷当上朋友，那相当于整个行政区…不，整个贝洛伯格的房产市场大门都会为我敞开！
t_en: If I could befriend Wallace, the doors of the real estate market in the Administrative District... no, the entire Belobog would be open for me!

t_ch: 这种天上掉下来的好机会，我怎么可能错过？
t_en: How could I miss such a good opportunity?

t_ch: 你先看看这张照片。
t_en: Please take a look at this photo first.

t_ch: 照片？什么照片？
t_en: Photo? What photo?

t_ch: …看完了，然后呢？这不就是张普通的照片吗？
t_en: I looked at it. What about it? Isn't this just an ordinary photo?

t_ch: 哦…我明白了，是莱姆士汀家打算收购黄金歌剧院，对不对？所以华劳斯公子才想和我交朋友，让我这个资深房地产中介帮他出谋划策？
t_en: Oh... I see. The Limesteins are planning to buy the Golden Theater, aren't they? That's why Mr. Wallace wants to make friends with me. He wants me, a senior real estate agent, to give him some professional advice.

t_ch: 不愧是贵族家的少爷，真是含蓄又不失优雅的表达方式啊…我等不及要和他见上一面了！
t_en: Mr. Wallace is indeed different. He uses a subtle and elegant way to convey that message... I can't wait to meet him!

t_ch: 我现在手头还有些工作要处理——麻烦你转告华劳斯公子，等我有空了就会联系他。
t_en: I still have some work to do. Please tell Mr. Wallace that I will contact him when I am free.

t_ch: 拉雯妮很想和你做朋友。
t_en: Lavonne would love to be your friend.

t_ch: …真的吗？终于有人愿意和我交朋友了？
t_en: Really? Finally someone wants to be friends with me...

t_ch: 你给她看过照片了吗？她怎么说？
t_en: Did you show her my photo? What did she say?

t_ch: 她说…
t_en: She said...

t_ch: 将和拉雯妮的谈话内容转述给了华劳斯……
t_en: You convey what Lavonne said to Wallace...

t_ch: 原来如此…那位叫拉雯妮的房地产中介，她是看中了我的家庭背景才打算和我交朋友的。
t_en: I see... Lavonne wants to be friends with me because of my family background.

t_ch: 我猜那位女士对音乐剧并不感兴趣…这种建立在功利之上的友谊，肯定算不上「真心」。我想我不得不拒绝她了。
t_en: It seems she is not interested in musicals... Friendships of utility are not true friendships. Looks like I have to refuse to be friends with her.

t_ch: 不过莱姆士汀家的人是不会轻易气馁的。我迟早会交到能和我共享爱好、有共同话题的朋友。
t_en: But we Limesteins are not quitters. Sooner or later, I will find a friend who has the same hobbies and interests as me.

t_ch: 感谢你的帮助，年轻人。这是你的报酬——下次我再需要帮忙的时候，希望你还在。
t_en: Thank you for your help. This is the payment I promised. I hope you can still help me the next time I need your help.

t_ch: 听说你又在找朋友了？
t_en: I heard you're looking for friends again?

t_ch: 没错，年轻人。我，华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，想继续在这茫茫人海中寻找真心朋友。
t_en: Yes. I, Wallace Cambridge Limestein, wish to continue to seek true friends.

t_ch: 我总结了过去的失败，得出一个结论：错不在我。
t_en: I've studied my failures and come to one conclusion: It's not my fault.

t_ch: 我的心中没有任何杂念，只想交一个真心朋友。反倒是我接触的那些人顾忌太多，或是对我的家世怀有疑问，或是对我抱持着无中生有的偏见。
t_en: I have no other intentions. I just want to make true friends. It was the people I came into contact with... They were too cautious. Some of them had suspicions about my family background, and some held prejudices against me for no reason.

t_ch: 我相信，只要继续把持莱姆士汀家真诚的待人之道，本人有朝一日必定能友遍天下。
t_en: I believe that I will one day make friends all over the world as long as I continue to treat people with sincerity like all the Limesteins.

t_ch: 我欣赏你的自信。
t_en: I admire your confidence.

t_ch: 你对「真诚」的定义很特别…
t_en: Your understanding of "sincerity" is pretty unusual...

t_ch: 干巴巴的讨论永远比不上实际行动。走吧，我已经物色好了今天的拍照地点。
t_en: Meaningless discussion can never be compared to real action. Come on, I've found a photo spot for today.

t_ch: 摄影需要故事方能吸引人的眼球，而站在你眼前的恰巧是一个有故事的男人——你要做的就是把我散发魅力的瞬间捕捉下来。
t_en: A photo needs a story to attract attention, and you're looking at a man full of stories. All you have to do is capture the moment when I exude charm.

t_ch: 来吧，来歌德宾馆的大堂找我。该让你看到华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀不一样的一面了。
t_en: Come and meet me in the lobby of the Goethe Hotel. It's time to show you a different side of me.

t_ch: 拍完了？让我看看……
t_en: Done? Let's have a look...

t_ch: 不错，不错…你抓住了精髓。歌德宾馆的红茶口感上乘，价格不菲。有心来此享用下午茶的客人必然都有着不俗的品位。
t_en: Good... This is a great photo. Goethe Hotel's black tea is excellent and expensive. Those who come here for afternoon tea must have good taste.

t_ch: 有机会的话，我想和自己未来的朋友一同享用下午茶的时光，在这舒适的大堂里相互推心置腹。
t_en: If there's ever a chance, I would love to enjoy afternoon tea and have a heart-to-heart talk with my future friend in the cozy lobby.

t_ch: 好了，该去帮我找朋友了。和以往一样，要找的人由我来指定。
t_en: All right, it's time to help me make friends. Just like before, I will decide who I want to make friends with.

t_ch: 你听过塔米拉这个名字吗？她是贝洛伯格当红的舞台剧明星。我一向希望能结交有天赋的艺人朋友，和他们聊天一定会大有收获。
t_en: Have you heard of Tamila? She is a popular stage actress in Belobog. I've always wanted to make friends with talented artists. Chatting with them will surely be rewarding.

t_ch: 你能否带着照片去帮我问问，看看她愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: Could you show the photo to her and ask her if she's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: …欸？华劳斯？
t_en: Huh...? Wallace?

t_ch: 我没听过这个名字，怎么这么突然？难道又是哪位疯狂的戏迷……
t_en: I've never heard of this name. This is all very sudden. Is he another crazy fan of mine...?

t_ch: 他是莱姆士汀家的公子。
t_en: He's a Limestein.

t_ch: 他很有钱。
t_en: He's rich.

t_ch: …这么说，他来自贝洛伯格的上流社会？
t_en: So, he is from the upper class of Belobog?

t_ch: 圈外人大概不了解，所有戏迷里最难缠的就是有钱有闲的那一类。抱歉，我不能答应他的请求……
t_en: People outside the theater industry might not understand that fans are hard to deal with, especially the ones with lots of money and free time. I'm sorry, but I can't be friends with him...

t_ch: 也许你该先看看这张照片。
t_en: Maybe you should look at this photo first.

t_ch: …照片？真是奇怪，一般的戏迷都更偏向于给我写信…好吧，让我看看。
t_en: Photo? That's weird. My fans usually write me letters. Well, I'll take a look.

t_ch: …啊，原来是这位！我经常在剧院的前排瞥见他，大概也能推测出他身世显赫……
t_en: Oh, it's him! I remember seeing him sitting in the front row of the theater a lot. He seems to be born into a noble family.

t_ch: 不行…那我就更不能和他发展私交了。我身为公众人物，又正处在事业上升期，万一在这个时候传出和名门望族的绯闻……
t_en: If that's the case, I can't be friends with him. As a public figure whose career is on the rise, I can't be seen getting involved with someone with a famous name...

t_ch: 麻烦你，能礼貌地帮我回绝莱姆士汀先生吗？如果他想要的话，我可以帮他在照片上签名，权当一点心意。
t_en: Would you be so kind as to tell Mr. Limestein that I can't be his friend? If he wants, I can sign his picture as a token of my gratitude.

t_ch: 塔米拉不想和你做朋友…
t_en: Tamila doesn't want to be your friend...

t_ch: …什么？她看过照片了吗？她有说什么吗？
t_en: What...? Has she seen the photo? Did she say anything?

t_ch: 她说…
t_en: She said...

t_ch: 将和塔米拉的谈话内容转述给了华劳斯……
t_en: You convey what Tamila said to Wallace...

t_ch: 原来如此…塔米拉小姐不想和我走得太近，是因为害怕自己的演艺生涯受挫——可以理解。
t_en: I see... Miss Tamila doesn't want to cozy up to me for fear of ruining her career. I can understand that.

t_ch: 可惜，本以为我能和她一起在歌德宾馆享用下午茶，畅聊演艺圈的趣闻轶事…看来这个愿望算是破灭了。
t_en: Too bad. I thought I could have afternoon tea with her at the Goethe Hotel and chat about the interesting things in the theater industry. I guess that will never happen.

t_ch: 不过莱姆士汀家的人是不会轻易气馁的。我迟早会交到「真心」的朋友，让拒我于门外的人后悔。
t_en: But we Limesteins are not quitters. Sooner or later, I will find a "true" friend, and those who rejected me will regret it.

t_ch: 感谢你的帮助，年轻人。这是你的报酬——下次我再需要帮忙的时候，希望你还在。
t_en: Thank you for your help. This is the payment I promised. I hope you can still help me the next time I need your help.

t_ch: 啊，我亲爱的孩子们。我听老朋友说起，你们找到了来自过去的歌德老宅，这些神奇造化，一定都是圣贤的安排。
t_en: Ah, my dear children. An old friend told me you found the Goethe Mansion from the past. These miracles must have been arranged by the Sage.

t_ch: 尽可能地找到了来自过去的典籍，它们是真的复制了过去吗？还是说，这只是破碎的残象而已。
t_en: Even if we find all books from the past, are they really copies of the past? Or is it just a shattered illusion?

t_ch: 啊，我亲爱的孩子们，你们做得好啊！
t_en: Ah, my dear children, you have all done well!

t_ch: 听说你需要帮助。
t_en: I hear you need help.

t_ch: 我只找到了这些。
t_en: I only found these.

t_ch: 听说你需要帮助。
t_en: I hear you need help.

t_ch: 你听说了啊？你也知道，我常来这里思考，看着眼前的垃圾桶，就像看着我自己，年近三十却一事无成。
t_en: Did you? You know, I often come here to contemplate life. When I am looking at that trashcan, I feel like I am looking at myself. I'm close to <unbreak>30</unbreak> but I haven't achieved anything in life.

t_ch: 前几天，我像往常一样来到这里，却发现垃圾桶里躺着一张漂亮的明信片——没想到竟然是寄给我的，署名是「垃圾大王」。
t_en: A few days ago, I came here like I always do, and I found a beautiful postcard lying in the trashcan. Surprisingly, it was addressed to me, it was from "Garbage King."

t_ch: 这位「垃圾大王」我也有所耳闻，听说是一位神秘的拾荒者。没人知道他的真实身份，可能是我一直待在垃圾桶边，引起了他的注意吧？
t_en: I've heard things about this "Garbage King." They say he's a mysterious scavenger and nobody knows who he really is. Maybe I've been hanging around this trashcan for so long that I caught his attention.

t_ch: 我原以为是自己误入别人的地盘，受到了警告…结果没想到，明信片上写的居然都是鼓励的话语。他说他看过我写的小说，很喜欢那些故事，希望我不要放弃。这可真是……
t_en: At first, I thought I trespassed somebody's territory and this was a warning... Unexpectedly, the postcard is full of encouragement. He said he's read my novel and he really loves my stories. He told me not to give up. It was really...

t_ch: 令人感动…
t_en: Touching...

t_ch: 十分可疑…
t_en: Suspicious...

t_ch: 不对…是令人绝望！
t_en: No... devastating!

t_ch: 也许他只是想安慰我，可字里行间，我看到的却是一位远比我更具才华的作家。
t_en: Maybe he's just trying to cheer me up. But from his words, I see a writer much more talented than myself.

t_ch: 你看——「一个人的诞生，就像垃圾落在垃圾桶中，发出悦耳动听的声音，令人心生喜悦」，多么精妙的比喻呀，我仿佛从中读出了古代战争年代的苦涩……
t_en: Look — "The birth of a person is like rubbish landing in the trashcan. There's a pleasant sound and it brings joy to people." What a wonderful metaphor. It's like I am reading about wars in the distant past...

t_ch: 还有这一句——「朋友，这一辈子太过漫长，你还有足够的时间去感受人生，直到贝洛伯格送别最后一个垃圾箱」，一个人要经历多少悲苦，才能写出如此浪漫的文字？
t_en: And this — "My friend, we have a long life ahead of us and you still have time to experience your life, until Belobog sends off the last trashcan." How much sorrow does one have to endure before one can write such romantic prose?

t_ch: 那些我苦思冥想才能写出的辞句，他却信手拈来，这种感觉就像是…我正在与一位遍历风雪的「国王」交谈，他如此伟岸，我才是那个渺小的流浪汉！
t_en: Words that take me ages to come up with seem so easy to him. I feel like... I am conversing with a "King" who conquered the wind and snow. Like he is a saint while I am just an insignificant vagrant!

t_ch: 我已经没心思想其他事了。如果你知道任何有关这位「垃圾之王」的事，请告诉我吧。
t_en: Now I can't focus on anything else. If you know anything about this "Garbage King," please let me know.

t_ch: 我想知道他是谁，从何而来，又经历过什么。至少，我想明白自己与对方的鸿沟落在哪里……
t_en: I want to know who he is, where he comes from and what he has been through. At least I want to understand the gap between us...

t_ch: 一个平平无奇的垃圾桶，就像一个平平无奇的垃圾桶。「垃圾大王」似乎不在这里。
t_en: An ordinary trashcan... Just like an ordinary trashcan. The "Garbage King" doesn't seem to be here.

t_ch: 一个空空如也的垃圾桶，似乎已被人光顾过。垃圾消失在垃圾桶里，就像水滴消失在水塘中。
t_en: An empty trashcan. Looks like it's been cleared out. Trash disappearing into a trashcan like droplets of water disappearing into a pond.

t_ch: 垃圾桶里躺着一张漂亮的明信片，上面写着：「朋友，你走在正确的路上，却走得太快；不妨暂且驻足，让时光得以歇息。署名：垃圾之王。」
t_en: A beautiful postcard is lying in the trashcan. It says, "My friend, you are walking the right path but you are walking too fast. Why don't you take a break and let time rest? Signed by: Garbage King."

t_ch: 这是你要的线索。
t_en: This is the clue you are looking for.

t_ch: 又一张明信片…不可思议，难道我的一举一动都在他的注视下吗？
t_en: Another postcard... Unbelievable. Has he been watching me?

t_ch: 如此奇妙的人，竟然是个街头的拾荒者…还是说，他其实另有身份，只是甘愿体会这种生活呢？
t_en: What a weirdo. I still can't believe he's a scavenger... Does he actually have another identity and is he doing this to experience another kind of life?

t_ch: 我感觉到了，好像有一簇灵感射入了我的大脑，一些我之前从未有过的想法…总之，谢谢你。我会好好珍惜这两张明信片的。
t_en: I can feel it. Inspirations shoot into my head. These are the ideas I've never had... Anyway, thank you. I will cherish these two postcards.

t_ch: 在垃圾桶里翻找「垃圾大王」…
t_en: Sifting through the trash to look for the Garbage King.

t_ch: 听说你需要帮助。
t_en: I hear you need help.

t_ch: 我需要帮助？有这回事吗…你等等啊，让我仔细回忆一下……
t_en: Help? Do I... Give me a minute, let me see if I can remember...

t_ch: …呃，你要帮我做什么？完了完了…昨天晚上到现在的记忆，我一点都没有了……
t_en: Um, help me with what? Oh no... I lost my memory from last night till now...

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 这下糟了，我也不知道啊！你还记得什么细节吗…比如从哪儿听说的，或是谁拜托的你……
t_en: That's too bad, me neither! Do you remember the details... Like where did you hear it or the person who told you?

t_ch: 也许…帮你找回记忆？
t_en: Maybe... helping you find your memory?

t_ch: 原来如此，这好像就说得通了……
t_en: I see, now it makes sense...

t_ch: 这样吧，要不…你在这附近转转，看看有没有什么奇怪的地方？要是我真的遇到了什么事，一定会留下痕迹的，没错吧？
t_en: Well... why don't you walk around here and see if you can find anything strange? If something did happen to me, there should be traces left behind, right?

t_ch: 这段时间，我也再努力回想一下，从头捋一遍…你沿着这条路一直走吧，然后往右…不对，是往左…不，也可能是那条路……
t_en: I'll also try my best to recall what happened right from the start... Walk along this way and turn right... No, turn left... Hold on, maybe it should be the other way...

t_ch: 你打开回收箱，看见垃圾袋向里凹陷，空出一个人的形状——是谁把这里当作了床？
t_en: You open the recycle bin and see a caved-in section among the bags of rubbish, in the shape of a person... Has someone been sleeping here?

t_ch: 半桶红色的油漆，已经快干了。一页白纸静静地躺在桶里，上面写着：「你永远无法逃离我。」
t_en: Half a bucket of red paint, which is almost dry. A white piece of paper lies silently in the bucket. It says, "you will never get away from me."

t_ch: 一把矿镐落在这里，木柄上刻着「马克西姆」。
t_en: A mining pick with name "Maxime" on the wooden handle is left here.

t_ch: 墙上有一些斑驳的痕迹，你隐约觉得似曾相识，却不知道这是什么。墙边的角落，痕迹仿佛勾勒出一个数字——「3」。
t_en: There are some dirt marks on the wall. They look familiar, but you can't understand what they mean. The marks seem to trace out a number — 3.

t_ch: 一块告示板，被人用炭笔粗暴地涂黑，只留下一个形似橄榄枝的图案，像是有人在磐岩镇的黑夜中记录着什么。
t_en: A notice board brutally covered in black soot, only leaving behind a shape that looks like an olive branch. It was as if someone was recording something in the dark Boulder Town night.

t_ch: 这应该和你有关。
t_en: This surely has something to do with you.

t_ch: 这是…矿镐？原来是这样，我刚刚也隐约想起来点什么，我好像是丢了什么重要的东西。
t_en: Is this... a mining pick? I see. I can vaguely remember something... I think I lost something quite important.

t_ch: 但这真的是我的矿镐吗？我不记得在上面刻过字啊…算了，想那么多干嘛，就当是它吧。
t_en: Is this really my mining pick? I don't remember carving my name on it... Anyway, does it matter?

t_ch: 不过，为什么会掉在那种地方呢…我得想一想，再好好想一想……
t_en: But why was it left there... Let me think... Let me think real hard...

t_ch: 听说你需要帮助。
t_en: I hear you need help.

t_ch: 呀，原来{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}是为这个来的吗，阿丽娜好开心哦~
t_en: Wow, did you come here for this? I am so happy.

t_ch: 是发生了一些事呢。最近，有奇怪的说法在诊所里传开了，说是…「只要把心意写在纸上，让信鸽带向天空，就能将声音传递给阿丽娜」什么的……
t_en: Yeah, something did happen. Recently a strange rumor has been circulating in the clinic that "If you write your wish on a piece of paper and let a pigeon carry it to the sky, your wish will be conveyed to Alina..."

t_ch: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}也觉得很奇怪，对不对？有什么话明明可以直接和阿丽娜说的呀，为什么要这么辛苦呢？
t_en: Don't you find this strange? They can just come to me directly. Why bother doing all that?

t_ch: 结果，街道上到处都是飞散的纸条。收到这么多信件，我当然很高兴啦，但也很担心会给大人们添麻烦。
t_en: As a result, there are now pieces of paper flying around the streets everywhere. Of course I am happy to get so many letters, but I'm also worried that this may become a problem for others.

t_ch: 阿丽娜不想看到大家挨罚。拜托了，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，可以在娜塔莎姐姐发现之前，帮我找到那只「信鸽」吗？
t_en: I don't want people to be punished. Can you please find that pigeon before Natasha finds out?

t_ch: 你远远地打量着菲多拉。有那么一瞬间，脑海中闪过了「信鸽」是机械造物的可能。但你很快否定了这个想法。
t_en: Looking at Fidora in the distance, an idea crosses your mind, the "pigeon" could be a robot. But you quickly rule out this idea.

t_ch: 你的目光扫过地面，试图捕捉羽毛和爪印的痕迹——结果当然一无所获。你从未在贝洛伯格见过飞翔的鸟类。
t_en: You look all over the ground, trying to find traces of feathers or claw marks — but you come up empty-handed, as expected. You have never seen a bird in Belobog, after all.

t_ch: 也许「信鸽」是某位斗士的名字。毕竟都有人叫「冷面小青龙」了，还有什么不可能的呢？
t_en: Maybe "pigeon" is the name of a warrior, which isn't that strange. After all, there's even somebody called "Cold Dragon Young."

t_ch: 一个纸团孤零零地躺在地上，它为什么会在这儿？你弯腰将其拾起，内心却有个声音让你不要打开——你应该把它带回给阿丽娜。
t_en: A paper ball is lying on the ground. Why is it here? You bend down to pick it up. Your inner voice tells you not to open it — you should bring it back to Alina.

t_ch: 我找到了这个。
t_en: I found this.

t_ch: 没有找到「信鸽」，但发现了奇怪的纸团？让我看看哦…咦，这是？
t_en: You didn't find the pigeon, but you found a strange paper ball? Let me see... is this...

t_ch: 原来是这样啊…我大概知道「信鸽」是什么了。{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，可以再帮我找找类似的纸团吗？我想，应该还有三个吧。
t_en: I see. I think I have an idea what the "pigeon" is now. {M#Sir}{F#Ma'am}, can you find more paper balls like this for me? I think there are three more.

t_ch: 至于这张纸上写了什么…算啦算啦，我还是替尤利安保守一下秘密吧~
t_en: As for what's written on it... Well, I think I better keep this a secret for Julian.

t_ch: 又一个纸团孤零零地躺在地上，你记忆中的磐岩镇并没有这么杂乱。
t_en: Another paper ball is lying on the ground. You didn't expect Boulder Town to be this messy.

t_ch: 一个纸团孤零零地藏在地髓之间，是纸团，总会被发现。
t_en: A paper ball is buried within the Geomarrow. Paper balls will always be found.

t_ch: 一个纸团孤零零地卡在路灯上，你轻轻一推，它掉了下来。
t_en: A ball of paper is stuck on the street lamp. You give it a soft push and it falls.

t_ch: 这些纸团够了吗？
t_en: Are these enough?

t_ch: 我看看哦。这是埃里克的，这是罗密欧的，还有这封，再加上之前尤利安的…大家的「信」都在这里啦。
t_en: Let me see. There's one from Eric, one from Romeo, now this one, plus the previous one from Julian... We've got everyone's "letters" here.

t_ch: 原来「让信鸽带向天空」就是「把信揉成团扔到天上去」的意思啊，真是的，至少折个纸飞机嘛。
t_en: So "let a pigeon carry it to the sky" just means "crumple up a paper ball and throw it into the air." Come on, can't they at least fold a paper airplane?
